articles.title: At Least 10 People Killed In Strikes On Gaza, Hospitals Say
articles.content:
代尔拜拉赫，加沙地带（美联社）——当地医院表示，以色列在加沙的空袭周四造成至少10名巴勒斯坦人死亡，此时世界大部分注意力都集中在以色列与黎巴嫩真主党之间的战斗上。

沙法医院表示，在加沙城，至少四次单独空袭造成9人死亡，该医院接收了遗体。医院表示，遇难者包括两名妇女和两名儿童。

红新月会运营的萨拉亚野战医院表示，加沙城另一次空袭在周四晚间造成至少一人死亡，另一人受伤。

其中一次空袭的画面显示，一栋看似住宅楼的建筑上层出现了一个巨大的洞。爆炸在内部墙壁上炸出孔洞，血迹斑斑的物品散落在房间内外。

在沙法医院，亲属们哀悼遇难者时，其中一名遇难者的叔叔瓦利德·谢比尔说：“他们说战争已经停止，但战争没有停止。每晚都有人被杀，我们有烈士。每一个早晨、每一个傍晚、每一个夜晚，这种杀戮对我们来说是持续不断的。”

以色列军方表示，加沙北部夜间空袭打死了四名哈马斯武装人员，称他们是负责保护哈马斯领导人并为其提供情报评估的机构的高级成员。军方表示，在空袭前采取了措施以降低平民受伤风险，包括使用精确弹药和空中监视。

目前尚不清楚晚间那次空袭的目标，军方尚未立即回应就此事的置评请求。

上周，以色列击毙了哈马斯最高军事领导人，而两周前的空袭已打死了他的前任。

**停火以来已有900多名巴勒斯坦人遇难**

自10月停火协议试图结束以色列与加沙地带巴勒斯坦激进组织哈马斯之间历时两年多的战争以来，这些死亡事件是最新发生的。虽然最激烈的战斗已经减弱，但脆弱的停火协议几乎每天都能听到以色列的炮火。

以色列军队进行了反复空袭，并频繁向靠近军事管制区的巴勒斯坦人开火，自停火生效以来已造成936人死亡，加沙卫生部表示。该卫生部是哈马斯领导政府的一部分，联合国机构和独立专家通常认为其数据可靠。该部门没有提供平民和武装人员死亡的分类数据。

激进分子对军队进行了枪击袭击，以色列表示其空袭是对停火违反或对其军队威胁的回应。停火以来，已有四名以色列士兵在加沙丧生。

以色列在加沙发动进攻是对哈马斯2023年10月袭击的回应，那次袭击造成约1200人死亡，另有251人被扣为人质。

** flotilla表示11名活动人士在利比亚被拘留**

全球苏穆德船队表示，至少11名试图引起国际社会关注以色列对加沙封锁的国际活动人士在试图通过陆路进入该地区期间，已在利比亚被拘留超过一周。

利比亚媒体报道称，这些活动人士因非法入境和缺乏许可证而被拘留。全球苏穆德组织者表示，所有参与者都持有有效签证。

该组织的海上船队由数十艘船组成，上个月在抵达加沙之前被拦截。数百名活动人士通过以色列和希腊被驱逐出境。以色列指责船队是一场“公关噱头”，携带的援助物资非常少。

超过200名医护人员和活动人士一直在尝试通过另一条陆路进入加沙。他们于5月15日离开毛里塔尼亚，一直前往埃及，准备通过拉法口岸进入加沙。

全球苏穆德表示，一名突尼斯国民于5月19日在距离利比亚-突尼斯边境约25公里（15英里）处被捕，当时他正在返回家园。另有10人于5月24日在利比亚沿海地中海沿岸、距突尼斯和埃及之间不到一半路程的锡特雷附近的一个检查站试图为车队谈判安全通道时被拘留。

该组织谴责这些拘留是“非法”和“任意”的，并表示利比亚于5月25日首次宣布拘留了这些活动人士——他们来自突尼斯、阿根廷、葡萄牙、意大利、美国、乌拉圭、波兰和西班牙。他们的拘留期限于周二再次延长10天。

__
美联社记者梅拉妮·利德曼和娜塔莉·梅尔策在以色列特拉维夫贡献报道。
articles.summary:

&

DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes in Gaza killed at least 10 Palestinians on Thursday, local hospitals said, even as much of the world’s attention was focused on the fighting between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon.

Nine people were killed in at least four separate strikes overnight in Gaza City, according to Shifa Hospital, which received the bodies. The hospital said the victims included two women and two children.

Advertisement

Another strike in Gaza City on Thursday evening killed at least one person and wounded another, according to Saraya Field Hospital, which is operated by the Red Crescent.

Footage of one of the strikes showed a massive hole in an upper floor in what appeared to be a residential apartment building. The blast blew holes through interior walls and scattered blood-stained belongings across the room and into the street.

“They say the war has stopped, but the war has not stopped,” said Walid Shbeir, the uncle of one of the men killed in the strikes, as relatives mourned the victims at Shifa Hospital. “Every night there is killing, and we have martyrs. Every night, in the morning, in the evening, and at night, this killing is continuous for us.”

Advertisement

Israel’s military said the overnight strikes in northern Gaza killed four Hamas militants, which it described as senior members of an apparatus responsible for protecting Hamas leaders and providing them with intelligence assessments. The military said steps were taken before the strikes to mitigate the risk of harm to civilians, including the use of precise munitions and aerial surveillance.

It was not clear what the evening strike was targeting, and the military did not immediately respond to a request for comment on it.

Advertisement

Last week, Israel killed the top Hamas military leader, two weeks after strikes that killed his predecessor.

More Than 900 Palestinians Killed Since Ceasefire Began

The fatalities were the latest in the coastal enclave since an October ceasefire deal attempted to halt a more than two-year war between Israel and the Palestinian militant Hamas group in Gaza. While the heaviest fighting has subsided, the fragile ceasefire has seen almost daily Israeli fire.

Advertisement

Israeli forces have carried out repeated airstrikes and frequently opened fire on Palestinians near military-held zones, killing 936 people since the ceasefire took effect, according to Gaza’s Health Ministry. The ministry, which is part of the Hamas-led government, is generally seen as reliable by United Nations agencies and independent experts. It does not give a breakdown of civilian and militant deaths.

Militants have carried out shooting attacks on troops, and Israel says its strikes are in response to violations of the truce or threats to its troops. Four Israeli soldiers have been killed in Gaza since the ceasefire.

Israel launched its offensive in Gaza in response to Hamas’ October 2023 attack that killed some 1,200 people, with 251 others taken hostage.

Advertisement

Flotilla Says 11 Activists Detained In Libya

At least 11 international activists attempting to bring attention to Israel’s blockade of Gaza have been detained in Libya for more than a week while trying to reach the territory by land, according to the Global Sumud Flotilla.

Libyan media reports said the activists were detained in connection with illegal entry and lack of permits. The Global Sumud organizers said the participants all had valid visas.

The organization’s maritime flotilla, consisting of dozens of boats, was intercepted last month before reaching Gaza. Hundreds of activists were deported via Israel and Greece. Israel accused the flotilla of being a “PR stunt” carrying very little amounts of aid.

Advertisement

More than 200 health workers and activists have been attempting a separate route overland to reach Gaza. They left Mauritania on May 15 and have been heading towards Egypt to enter Gaza via the Rafah crossing.

Global Sumud said a Tunisian national was arrested on May 19 around 25 kilometers (15 miles) from the Libya-Tunisia border while returning home. Another 10 people were detained on May 24 while trying to negotiate safe passage for their convoy at a checkpoint near Sitre, in Libya, along the Mediterranean coast less than halfway between Tunisia and Egypt.

The organization, which has condemned the detentions as “unlawful” and “arbitrary,” said Libya first announced it was holding the activists — who hail from Tunisia, Argentina, Portugal, Italy, the U.S., Uruguay, Poland, and Spain — on May 25. Their detention was extended on Tuesday by another 10 days.

Advertisement

__

Associated Press writers Melanie Lidman and Natalie Melzer contributed from Tel Aviv, Israel.

Related
IsraelPalestine gaza stripLibyafreedom flotilla

articles.title: Marjane Satrapi, Iranian-French Author Of Graphic Novel 'Persepolis', Dies Aged 56
articles.content:
巴黎6月4日（路透社）——伊朗裔法国艺术家、电影制片人和自传体漫画小说《我在伊朗长大》作者玛嘉·莎塔碧周四去世，享年56岁。爱丽舍宫发表声明称：“她的离去标志着法国文化界一位杰出人物的消逝，也是一位热爱自由的艺术家的逝去。她的作品传递了普世信息，使她赢得了极高的国际声誉。”

莎塔碧家人通过法国新闻社发表的声明称，她在丈夫马蒂亚斯·里帕去世一年多后因“悲伤”离世。里帕是瑞典演员、制片人和编剧。除此以外，未公布更多死因详情。

莎塔碧1969年出生于德黑兰，成长于一个左倾家庭。十几岁时父母送她前往维也纳，后返回伊朗学习美术，随后定居法国继续在斯特拉斯堡深造。

她以《我在伊朗长大》这部黑白自传漫画回溯了自己的革命、流亡与回归经历，描绘了1979年伊斯兰革命前后动荡的童年。该书成为国际畅销书，后被改编为动画电影，荣获戛纳评审团奖并获奥斯卡提名。

莎塔碧的作品融合政治抗争与黑色幽默，以极简视觉风格著称，成为同时代最知名的漫画小说家之一。她随后执导了《梅子鸡》、《声音》以及关于科学家居里夫人的《放射性》等影片。

莎塔碧还为巴黎2024年奥运会设计了一幅9米长的羊毛三联画，展示运动员在埃菲尔铁塔旁的竞技场景。

莎塔碧同时成为流亡者、女性自由和威权主义的坚定发声者，经常利用其公众平台谴责伊朗的压迫行径。

2025年，她拒绝接受法国最高荣誉勋章——荣誉军团勋章，理由是法国对伊朗的“虚伪态度”。她当时写道：“我无法继续看着伊朗权贵阶层的孩子们来法国度假，甚至获得法国国籍，而与此同时，年轻异见者却难以获得旅游签证来看看这个启蒙与人权之国是什么样子。”
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

PARIS, June 4 (Reuters) - Marjane Satrapi, the Iranian-French artist, filmmaker and author of the autobiographical graphic novel “Persepolis”, has died aged 56, French President Emmanuel Macron’s office said on Thursday.

“Her passing is that of a figure of French culture and of an artist enamored of freedom, whose work carried a universal message and had earned her immense international renown,” the Elysee said in a statement.

Advertisement

A statement released by members of her family to the French news agency AFP said she had died of “sadness” a little over a year after the death of her husband, Swedish actor, producer and screenwriter Mattias Ripa. No further information about the cause of her death was available.

Born in 1969, Satrapi spent her childhood in Tehran in a communist-leaning household. Her parents sent her to Vienna as a teenager, before she returned to Iran to study fine arts and later settled in France, where she continued her training in Strasbourg.

Advertisement

She drew on that life of revolution, exile and return in “Persepolis”, the stark black-and-white memoir that chronicled her childhood during and after Iran’s 1979 Islamic Revolution. The book became an international success and was later adapted into an animated film, which won the jury prize at Cannes and was nominated for an Academy Award.

Satrapi’s work mixed political defiance with dark humour and a stripped-down visual style, making her one of the best-known graphic novelists of her generation. She went on to direct films including “Chicken with Plums”, “The Voices” and “Radioactive”, about scientist Marie Sklodowska Curie.

Satrapi also designed a nine-metre wool triptych for the Paris 2024 Olympics, showing athletes competing around the Eiffel Tower.

Advertisement

Satrapi also became a prominent voice on exile, women’s freedom and authoritarianism, frequently using her public platform to denounce repression in Iran.

In 2025, she refused the Legion of Honour, France’s top order of merit, citing France’s “hypocritical attitude” toward Iran, French media reported.

“I can’t continue seeing the children of Iranian oligarchs come to spend their holidays in France, even become naturalized, while at the same time young dissidents have difficulty in obtaining a tourist visa to come to see what the country of the Enlightenment and human rights looks like,” she wrote at the time.

Advertisement

(Reporting by Hugo LhomedetEditing by Peter Graff)

Related
Marjane SatrapiPersepolis

articles.title: Iranian-French Cartoonist And Filmmaker Marjane Satrapi Has Died At 56
articles.content:
巴黎（美联社）——备受赞誉的伊朗裔法国漫画家和电影导演玛嘉·萨特拉比（Marjane Satrapi）于周四去世，享年56岁。法国总统府发表了这一声明。

“她的离去标志着法国文化界失去了一位领军人物，以及一位献身于自由的艺术家。她的作品传递了普世信息，使她在国际范围内获得了巨大声誉，”法国总统府在声明中表示。

声明称，马克龙总统及其夫人“向一位杰出的艺术家致敬，她将伊朗童年转化为一部普世寓言”。

据法国新闻电视台BFM TV和其他法国媒体报道，萨特拉比“死于悲伤”，距她丈夫、瑞典电影制片人兼演员马蒂亚斯·里帕（Mattias Ripa）去世仅一年多，据与她亲近的人士发表的声明称。

法国美术学院（她于2024年当选为该院院士）在一份社交媒体声明中表达了深切哀悼，称她为“电影和电影教育的热情倡导者”，并提到她今年创立了一个基金会，帮助国际学生来巴黎学习电影。

萨特拉比最为人熟知的是她的黑白自传漫画书和电影《我在伊朗长大》（Persepolis），这部作品讲述了她在伊朗出生和成长的故事，以伊斯兰革命为时代背景。

《我在伊朗长大》于2007年在戛纳电影节获得影评人协会大奖，2008年获得法国凯撒奖最佳改编剧本奖，并获得2008年奥斯卡最佳动画长片提名。

2007年在戛纳接受美联社采访时，萨特拉比表示，这部讲述她在德黑兰作为马克思主义知识分子倔强女儿生活的电影，旨在提醒世人伊朗人和所有人一样。

她说：“我们想说的是，如果这些人让你感到恐惧，请仔细看看：他们有父母，有爱人，有希望，有故事。”

当时伊朗当局抗议这部电影入选戛纳电影节，并向法国驻德黑兰大使馆致信。

萨特拉比于1969年11月22日出生于伊朗拉什特，但由于1979年革命后国内极端主义盛行，她的父母于1983年将她送往奥地利维也纳完成学业。

但萨特拉比发现奥地利对她充满敌意，她非常想念父母，于1989年回到伊朗，进入德黑兰大学学习视觉传达专业并获得学位。

毕业时，萨特拉比决定自己终于准备好离开伊朗，接受父母十年前拼命想给她的机会。1994年，她移居法国，先在斯特拉斯堡学习，后来搬到巴黎。

她的漫画作品还包括《刺绣》（Broderies）和《梅子鸡》（Poulet aux prunes），后者也被改编成电影。作为电影导演，她执导了多部作品，包括《约塔帮》（La Bande des Jotas）和《放射性》（Radioactive），后者是一部关于波兰物理学家居里夫人的传记片。

萨特拉比于2023年与一群艺术家和学者共同策划出版了《女性、生命、自由》（Femme, vie, liberté）一书，描绘了2022年玛莎·阿米尼（Mahsa Amini）在所谓“道德警察”手中死亡后伊朗发生的抗议活动。该基金会表示，这部作品谴责了伊朗社会、尤其是女性在伊朗政权统治下遭受的压迫和缺乏人权。

萨特拉比于2024年当选法国美术学院院士。同年，法国政府还向她授予法国最高荣誉——荣誉军团勋章，但她拒绝了，理由是法国在支持伊朗人民争取民主的斗争方面做得不够。

她在2025年1月致法国当局的信中写道：“支持伊朗女性的革命不能仅仅停留在照片或演讲上。当人们为民主而战时，我们应该支持他们。”

2024年，萨特拉比获得西班牙阿斯图里亚斯公主基金会颁发的传播与人文奖。该组织表示，她是“人权和自由捍卫事业中不可或缺的声音”。评委们将她描述为“女性领导的公民参与的象征”。

萨特拉比的丈夫于2025年4月去世，享年53岁。在她的Instagram页面上，一系列帖子中只留下了一条消息：“因为我失去了我此生的挚爱。”
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

PARIS (AP) — Acclaimed Iranian-French cartoonist and filmmaker Marjane Satrapi, a prominent advocate for women’s rights, has died at 56, the French presidency said Thursday.

“Her passing marks the loss of a leading figure of French culture and an artist devoted to freedom, whose work carried a universal message and earned her immense international acclaim,” the French presidency said in a statement.

Advertisement

President Emmanuel Macron and his wife “pay tribute to a remarkable artist who transformed an Iranian childhood into a universal fable,” the statement said.

News broadcaster BFM TV and other French media reported Satrapi has “died of sadness” a little over a year after the death of her husband, Swedish film producer and actor Mattias Ripa, according to a statement from people close to the artist.

Advertisement

The French Academy of Fine Arts, of which she was a member, expressed its deep sadness in a social media statement, paying tribute to “a passionate advocate for cinema and film education” who earlier this year created a foundation to help international students come to Paris to study film.

Satrapi is best-known for her monochrome autobiographical comic book and film “Persepolis,” a coming-of-age tale set against the Islamic Revolution in her native Iran.

“Persepolis” won the Film Critics Grand Prix at the Cannes Festival in 2007 and the César Award for Best Adapted Screenplay in 2008, in addition to being nominated for Best Animated Feature at the 2008 Oscars.

Advertisement

The film, which details her life in Tehran as the willful daughter of intellectual Marxists, is a reminder that Iranians are just like everyone else, Satrapi told The Associated Press in a 2007 interview in Cannes.

“What we wanted to say is, if these people scare you, look closer: They have parents, they have lovers, they have hope, they have stories,” she said.

Advertisement

Iranian authorities at the time protested the movie’s inclusion at Cannes, sending a letter to the French Embassy in Tehran.

Satrapi was born on Nov. 22, 1969, in Rasht, Iran, but her parents sent her to Vienna, Austria, in 1983 to finish her studies because of the extremism in their country following the 1979 Revolution that brought Ayatollah Khomeini to power.

But Satrapi, who found Austria hostile and who desperately missed her parents, returned to Iran in 1989 to attend Tehran University, where she earned a degree in visual communications.

Advertisement

By the time she graduated, Satrapi decided she finally was ready to leave Iran and accept the opportunities her parents had been so desperate to give her a decade before. In 1994 she moved to France. She studied in Strasbourg and later moved to Paris.

Her graphic novels also include “Broderies” (“Embroideries”) and “Poulet aux prunes” (“Chicken with plums”), which also was adapted into a film. As a filmmaker, she has directed several works including “La Bande des Jotas” (“The Gang of Jotas”) and “Radioactive” (“Madame Curie”), a biography about the Polish physicist Marie Curie.

Satrapi in 2023 coordinated the book “Femme, vie, liberté” (“Woman, Life, Freedom”) together with a group of artists and academics to illustrate the revolts that occurred in Iran after the death of Mahsa Amini in 2022 at the hands of the so-called “morality police.” The work denounces the repression and lack of human rights that Iranian society, especially women, suffers at the hands of the Iranian regime, the foundation said.

Advertisement

Satrapi was elected member of the French Academy of Fine Arts in 2024. She also was offered France’s highest award, the Legion of Honor, that same year but declined it, arguing France was not doing enough to support Iranian people fighting for democracy.

“Supporting the women’s revolution in Iran cannot be reduced to photos or speeches,” she wrote in a January 2025 letter to French authorities. “When people are fighting for democracy, we should support them.”

In 2024, Satrapi won the Princess of Asturias Foundation award in Spain for communication and humanities. The organization said she was “an essential voice in the defense of human rights and freedom.” The judges described her as “a symbol of civic engagement led by women.”

Advertisement

Satrapi’s husband died in April 2025 at 53. On her Instagram page, only one message was left in a series of posts: “Because I have lost the love of my life.”

Related

Marjane Satrapi

For The First Time, House Votes To End Iran War In Rebuke To Trump
Trump Says Islamic State Group Leader Was Killed In A Joint U.S.-Nigerian Mission
Trump Just Made A Bold Comment About Religion And Crime — And Experts Have THOUGHTS
